# Magistr and Maps

> Deep thoughts, notes, reposts and thinking aloud bout IT, architecture and maps.

Magistr and Maps is a personal blog by Magistr covering IT infrastructure, DevOps, systems thinking, and AI-driven development.

## Blog Posts

- [Owning my own heartbeat](https://magistr.me/blog/owning-my-own-heartbeat.md): Building a self-hosted health pipeline — Pixel Watch → Google Health API → VictoriaMetrics → Grafana: the data shapes, the probe map, and what to ask the API for.
- [Reading the board from the stands](https://magistr.me/blog/reading-the-board-from-the-stands.md): I ignored Nvidia for two years. Then two interviews flipped me — on Jensen as the best Wardley strategist alive, and on what I actually wanted while watching him.
- [Nvidia, Oxide, and tinygrad through a Wardley lens — the long version](https://magistr.me/blog/nvidia-oxide-tinygrad-wardley-lens.md): The long version: three AI-compute value chains mapped and ranked — Jensen the strategist, Oxide's durable niche, tinygrad's right target and wrong method — plus the bezzle counter-thesis and a live co-design exhibit.
- [swamp-go-brr, the brain](https://magistr.me/blog/swamp-go-brr-the-brain.md): Why the autonomous agent loop inside the Firecracker cage is a deterministic state machine and not a swarm: how gobrr decomposes, schedules, and gates the work, plus what held and what bit me building it.
- [Build the cage first](https://magistr.me/blog/build-the-cage-first.md): Why I built a Firecracker microVM extension before the autonomous agent loop that needs it: the lethal trifecta, the painful microVM build, and a loop that shipped a real swamp extension end to end.
- [The shape of the decision](https://magistr.me/blog/the-shape-of-the-decision.md): Why the ABN affordability number isn't the real number for an expat in the Netherlands — and how I turned a fuzzy apartment-and-mortgage decision into a plan I can actually steer, with good-planning and Bovolon's four layers.
- [A feed to think with](https://magistr.me/blog/a-feed-to-think-with.md): How I turned a noisy Amsterdam apartment feed into a clean, high-signal one I can reason with — good-planning underneath, a move.nl dossier on top, public-data enrichment, and my requirements encoded as filters.
- [JSCAD CAD Generation with Swamp and Claude Code: Setup Guide](https://magistr.me/blog/jscad-swamp-setup-guide.md): Step-by-step guide: install Swamp from scratch, set up @magistr/jscad-cad extensions, configure the Claude Code skill, and generate 3D models from text descriptions.
- [Building a CAD Pipeline with JSCAD, Swamp, and Claude](https://magistr.me/blog/jscad-cad-generation-with-swamp-and-claude.md): How we built a CAD generation pipeline using JSCAD, Swamp extension models, and Claude — from choosing the right CAD system, through 7 iterations of debugging a genie lamp with 6-view projections, to publishing extensions and finding a bundle cache bug.
- [Today's Session: Cozystack on Tapok Cluster](https://magistr.me/blog/cozystack-on-tapok-cluster.md): Deploying Cozystack on a Talos Kubernetes cluster using Swamp extension models, with claude-code handling the execution while the author approves steps.
- [Running macOS Tiger on QEMU: A Technical Post-Mortem](https://magistr.me/blog/running-macos-tiger-on-qemu.md): A 10-hour technical post-mortem of attempting to virtualize macOS Tiger 10.4 on QEMU with OpenCore, managed by claude-code and Swamp on Unraid.
- [The Sight of Systems](https://magistr.me/blog/the-sight-of-systems.md): From observability dashboards to moldable development tools, exploring why we need the same 'sight' into our code that Grafana gives us into running systems.
- [The Pain-Driven Workflow](https://magistr.me/blog/the-pain-driven-workflow.md): Walking through the painfully slow cycle of developing a Terraform module to show why engineers need better foundations, inspired by System Initiative's approach.
- [A dream of AI-DLC](https://magistr.me/blog/a-dream-of-ai-dlc.md): A manifesto for the AI-Driven Development Lifecycle, reimagining how we build software when AI handles the coding and humans focus on intent and architecture.
- [A future ways of working](https://magistr.me/blog/future-ways-of-working.md): Bryan Cantrill's 2013 vision of leaderless engineering culture, how Oxide Computer builds on those principles, and what it means for the future of work in tech.
- [CfgMgmtCamp Day 0](https://magistr.me/blog/cfgmgmtcamp-day-0.md): Reflections from Config Management Camp and FOSDEM, finding a tribe in the English-speaking DevOps community, and the value of in-person tech meetups.
- [Devops Landscape](https://magistr.me/blog/devops-landscape.md): Exploring the shifting DevOps landscape and mapping the current state of tooling and practices to understand where the industry is heading.
- [Devops Roadmap](https://magistr.me/blog/devops-roadmap.md): A practical DevOps roadmap using Wardley Maps, covering Agile, Lean, and Six Sigma stages with relevant tools and technologies for each phase.

## Optional

- [About](https://magistr.me/me/me): About the author
- [RSS Feed](https://magistr.me/blog/index.xml): RSS feed for blog posts
- [Full Content for LLMs](https://magistr.me/llms-full.txt): Complete blog content in a single file
